channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-21
item.description:
Our open-source research community has been bustling with activity across multiple projects. Notably, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has released version `v2.4.95`, which includes a new feature to flatten fields for non-clinical result tables, while also updating documentation to reflect cross-session skill-file commit coordination. Meanwhile, the `Davidvandijcke/coarse` project has made significant progress on localization, with the confirmation email and review page now supporting multiple languages. The `JamieCummins/regcheck` project has also seen improvements, including enhancements to the "qwen" feature and logging functionality, as well as efforts to debug an OSF bug. These projects demonstrate the power of collaboration and iterative development, and we celebrate the progress made by our community members. As these projects continue to evolve, we look forward to seeing the connections and synergies that emerge between them.

121 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck37 commits
docpluck31 commits
coarse30 commits
metacheck20 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: docpluck: 5 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a feature was added to flatten fields for non-clinical result tables as part of `REQUEST_11` in version `v2.4.95`. The documentation was also updated to log the shipment of `v2.4.95` and `REQUEST_11`, as well as to note deferred follow-ups including "effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat". Additionally, the documentation was updated to record the cross-session skill-file commit coordination, which is now fulfilled, and to reflect prior-session housekeeping including the `REQUEST_10` customer broadcast and deploy smoke-gate fix. Furthermore, the library and application version-pin sync was enforced through a directive and QA/review/deploy gate.

5 commit(s) today:

  • 2dbdd98 [main] docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The specific change made was the addition of a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned. There were no changes to existing modules or addition of new ones, aside from the added link. The commit message does not mention any other areas of the repository being modified. The work was limited to a single commit on the `main` branch.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
